title: lupinus eriocalyx
intro: Lupinus eriocalyx, also known as the 'Blue Spurred Lupin', is a perennial herb native to Western Australia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where the plant's roots and leaves are used to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Lupinus eriocalyx can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it accessible for those looking to harness its potential health benefits.
